title: ai-harness-toolset reviewer config
description: Schema and key reference for config/reviewer.json. The reviewer config file itself must stay pure JSON with no comments; this adjacent schema carries the per-key documentation. Only currently implemented runtime keys are described. Per-key resolution: 'model' is REQUIRED and has NO built-in fallback — review-run.ps1 and review-safety-negtest.ps1 fail-fast if it is absent or empty (a concrete model version is tied to an external lifecycle and must come from this source-of-truth; precedence: explicit -Model CLI parameter > this value > fail-fast). 'reasoningEffort' is optional and resolves explicit -Effort CLI parameter > this value > policy safe-default 'xhigh'.

model.description: Reviewer model. REQUIRED. Read by review-run.ps1 (and review-safety-negtest.ps1) and passed to the Codex CLI as --model. Precedence: explicit -Model CLI parameter > this value > fail-fast. There is NO built-in model default/fallback: a concrete model version is tied to an external lifecycle, so an absent/empty model fails fast rather than being masked by a hardcoded version. fallbackModel is not auto-used.

reasoningEffort.description: Per-invocation reviewer reasoning effort. Read by review-run.ps1 and passed to the Codex CLI as -c model_reasoning_effort=<value> when invoking the reviewer (enforced as of Batch B of the review-system polishing implementation plan; previously metadata-only). Precedence: explicit -Effort CLI parameter > this value > built-in safe default 'xhigh'. The safe default is 'xhigh' per the adopted decision record (docs/systems/review/REVIEW_POLISHING_DECISION_RECORD.md); only clearly-simple local-correctness packets downgrade via an explicit -Effort. An out-of-enum value fails fast in review-run (no silent fallback). This config-driven enforcement does not by itself establish U9 operational, which also requires the Batch C reviewer-safety verification.
